heroHeadline: PLA Pulls Back to 2 Sorties Apr 24 After Anniversary Spike — 7 PLAN Vessels Elevated; Defense Budget Talks Stall; TSMC Hits Record High; 20 Days to Trump-Xi Summit
heroSubtitle: Taiwan's MND detected 2 PLA aircraft sorties and 7 PLAN vessels on April 24, 2026 — a sharp air de-escalation from the 15-sortie PLA Navy 77th anniversary spike of April 23, but with elevated naval presence (7 vessels, highest since the April 20 Liaoning carrier transit). The April 24 pullback continues the oscillating spike-and-retreat pattern: 24 sorties (Apr 21) → 6 sorties (Apr 22) → 15 sorties (Apr 23, Navy anniversary) → 2 sorties (Apr 24). The elevated 7-vessel PLAN posture sustains maritime pressure even as air sorties drop. Taiwan's cross-party defense budget negotiations stalled again April 24, with KMT holding at NT$800B vs. the cabinet's NT$1.25T; INDOPACOM Admiral Paparo warned legislators they cannot 'starve the chicken' on defense spending. TSMC shares surged to an all-time high after Taiwan relaxed single-stock investment caps for funds, and the company announced its A13 chip for 2029 volume production. With 20 days remaining before the Trump-Xi Beijing summit (May 14-15), the $14B PAC-3/NASAMS arms package remains on hold, and analysts assess Beijing is continuing its dual-track pressure-and-diplomacy strategy — calibrating air sorties to avoid sustained diplomatic liability while maintaining persistent naval presence around Taiwan.
footerNote: Data sourced from Taiwan MND, US DoD, DSCA, CSIS ChinaPower, Global Taiwan Institute, AIT, Reuters, AP, Bloomberg, CNBC, NPR, Taipei Times, Focus Taiwan, Nikkei Asia, Japan Times, ANI News, Tribune India, Vision Times, South China Morning Post, Taiwan News, Asia Times, The Asia Cable, GlobalSecurity.org, Xinhua, CGTN, The Diplomat, Washington Times, Global Times, People's Daily Online, Al Jazeera, The Manila Times, CNBC, MarineLink, Stars and Stripes, US News & World Report, Free Malaysia Today, Military Times, and institutional analyses. All claims reflect multi-pole sourcing across Western, East Asian, and international media.
